title: FxPro Pricing Explained: All Plans, Costs & Fees (2026)

excerpt: Complete breakdown of FxPro pricing — all plans, hidden fees, and how to save money.
content:
FxPro Pricing Overview

FxPro operates a commission-free account opening model—all trading accounts cost $0 per month to open and maintain. There are no monthly subscription fees, platform fees, or hidden account charges. Instead, FxPro makes money through spreads and, on certain account types, per-trade commissions. This zero-cost entry point makes FxPro an accessible option for new and experienced forex traders alike, though your actual trading costs will depend on which platform and account type you choose.

The broker offers four distinct trading platforms—MT4, MT5, cTrader, and the proprietary FxPro Edge—each available at no cost. The only potential ongoing charge is an inactivity fee applied after 6 months of no trading activity, which encourages active use of the platform.

All FxPro Plans Compared

FxPro structures its offering around four primary trading platforms rather than traditional "tiers." Each platform is free to open, but they differ in execution model, spreads, and available features:

Platform Monthly Cost Spreads Best For
Demo Account $0/mo Live market data (simulated trades) Learning & strategy testing
MT4/MT5 Standard $0/mo Higher spreads (variable) Retail traders, EAs, indicators
cTrader Raw $0/mo + per-trade commission Raw spreads from 0.0 pips High-volume traders, scalpers
FxPro Edge $0/mo Proprietary pricing model Multi-asset CFD traders

Demo Account ($0/mo): Perfect for new traders. You get $100,000 in virtual funds, real-time market data, and the ability to test strategies risk-free. The demo account mirrors live market conditions but with no real capital at risk. Ideal for learning the platform and backtesting strategies before going live.

MT4/MT5 Standard ($0/mo): FxPro's entry-level live trading option. You can trade forex, indices, commodities, stocks, and crypto with access to Expert Advisors and custom indicators. Spreads are higher than raw accounts, but there's no per-trade commission. Good for traders who prefer simplicity and don't need ultra-tight spreads.

cTrader Raw ($0/mo + commission): For serious traders seeking the tightest execution. Raw spreads start from 0.0 pips on major pairs, but you'll pay a per-trade commission (exact rates not specified in available data—verify directly with FxPro). cTrader offers advanced order types, algorithmic trading via cAlgo, and professional-grade tools. Best if you trade frequently and can offset commission costs with tighter spreads.

FxPro Edge ($0/mo): FxPro's proprietary platform combining STP/ECN execution with their own pricing model. Supports automated trading and offers access to 2,100+ instruments. A solid middle ground between Standard and Raw accounts—lower spreads than MT4/MT5 without cTrader's per-trade commission.

Free Plan / Free Trial

FxPro offers a genuine free demo account with no limitations on duration. You get:

  • $100,000 in virtual funds
  • Access to all platforms (MT4, MT5, cTrader, Edge)
  • Real-time market data and trading conditions
  • Full feature access including Expert Advisors and indicators
  • Ability to test strategies indefinitely before risking real money

This is genuinely valuable. You can spend weeks or months paper trading, backtesting, and learning before funding a live account. Many brokers limit demo accounts to 30 days; FxPro's unlimited demo removes that pressure and is worth taking full advantage of.

To move to a live account, you'll need to make a deposit. Minimum deposit amounts are not specified in the available data—contact FxPro directly to confirm minimums for your preferred platform.

Hidden Costs and Fees

While FxPro advertises zero monthly fees, several costs can impact your actual trading expenses:

  • Spreads: Your primary cost. MT4/MT5 Standard accounts have variable spreads (exact ranges not detailed). cTrader Raw accounts have spreads from 0.0 pips but add per-trade commissions. FxPro Edge offers intermediate spreads.
  • Commissions (cTrader Raw): If you use the Raw account, you'll pay per-trade commissions. Rates depend on your trading volume and pair—request a rate sheet from FxPro.
  • Inactivity Fee: After 6 months of no trading activity, FxPro charges an inactivity fee (amount not specified in available data). If you're a long-term holder who doesn't actively trade, check the fee structure before opening an account.
  • Withdrawal Fees: Not specified in available data. Some brokers charge withdrawal fees; verify FxPro's policy before funding.
  • Deposit Methods: Credit cards, bank transfers, and e-wallets may have different fees depending on your payment method and location. These are typically charged by your bank, not FxPro, but factor them in.
  • Overnight Holding Costs (Swap Rates): If you hold positions overnight, FxPro charges/credits swap rates based on interest rate differentials. Not a "hidden" fee, but a real cost for position traders.
  • Data Feeds: Premium market data or specialized feeds may cost extra (not detailed in available data).

The bottom line: FxPro's zero monthly fee is genuine, but actual trading costs depend entirely on your account type, trading frequency, and position-holding habits.

FxPro Pricing vs Competitors

How does FxPro stack up against other major forex brokers? Note: Competitor pricing below is approximate based on typical market rates and should be verified directly with each broker.

Broker Monthly Fee Minimum Spread (approx.) Key Differentiator
FxPro $0 0.0 pips (Raw) Multiple platforms, global regulation
Interactive Brokers (approx.) $0–$20/mo 0.2–0.5 pips Higher minimums, stocks + forex
Oanda (approx.) $0 1–2 pips Retail-focused, easy onboarding
IG (approx.) $0 0.6–1.5 pips Wide instrument range, education

FxPro's Competitive Position:

  • vs. Oanda: FxPro's cTrader Raw account (0.0 pips) beats Oanda's typical 1–2 pip spreads, but only if you can absorb the per-trade commission. For casual traders, Oanda may be cheaper.
  • vs. Interactive Brokers: FxPro has lower spreads on forex and lower barriers to entry. Interactive Brokers suits traders who want stocks + forex integration.
  • vs. IG: Both have zero monthly fees, but FxPro offers tighter raw spreads and more platform variety (MT4, MT5, cTrader, Edge vs. IG's proprietary platform).

Value Proposition: FxPro wins on platform choice and spread tightness (especially cTrader Raw), but you'll pay commissions on Raw accounts. For high-volume scalpers, this is a win. For casual traders, the MT4/MT5 Standard or FxPro Edge accounts may offer better value than competing brokers with higher spreads.

Is FxPro Worth the Price?

FxPro is worth it if you:

  • Want multiple platform options (MT4, MT5, cTrader, proprietary Edge)
  • Trade forex, indices, commodities, or crypto—they support all four
  • Use automated trading (Expert Advisors, cAlgo scripting)
  • Trade frequently enough to justify cTrader Raw commissions with 0.0 pip spreads
  • Value robust regulation (FCA, CySEC, FSCA) and a London-based, established firm (since 2006)
  • Want backtesting, performance analytics, and educational resources built in

FxPro may not be ideal if you:

  • Are based in the United States (FxPro is not available to US residents)
  • Are a passive long-term position holder (the inactivity fee penalizes non-trading accounts)
  • Prefer a single, simple platform over multiple options
  • Want hand-holding customer support on weekends (limited on weekends per the available data)

Plan Recommendations:

  • Beginners: Start with the demo account (free, unlimited duration). Once ready, open MT4/MT5 Standard for simplicity and EA support.
  • Intermediate Traders: Evaluate FxPro Edge or MT5 Standard. If spreads are a bottleneck, cTrader Raw may pay for itself through tighter execution.
  • Professional/Algorithmic Traders: cTrader Raw with cAlgo automation or FxPro Edge for advanced order types and tight spreads.
  • High-Volume Scalpers: cTrader Raw (0.0 pip spreads) likely offers the best cost-per-trade despite commissions.

How to Save on FxPro

FxPro's zero monthly fee structure leaves little room for traditional discounts, but you can minimize costs:

  • Use the Demo Account First: Spend time testing your strategy on the demo before going live. This reduces your risk of costly mistakes on live capital.
  • Choose the Right Account Type: Don't pay cTrader Raw commissions unless your spread savings exceed the commission cost. For most traders, MT4/MT5 Standard or Edge is sufficient.
  • Maintain Trading Activity: The inactivity fee kicks in after 6 months of no trades. If you're holding long-term, make at least one trade every 6 months to avoid the fee, or confirm the exact fee amount and consider other brokers if it's significant.
  • Leverage API Access: If you're algorithmic, use FxPro's API to automate trading. This can improve fill quality and consistency, reducing slippage costs.
  • Monitor Swap Rates: If holding overnight positions, be aware of swap rates. Plan your position sizing and holding periods to minimize overnight carry costs.
  • Verify Deposit Methods: Use bank transfers or methods with lower fees (avoid high-fee credit cards if possible), though deposit fees are typically charged by your bank, not FxPro.

Discount Codes & Promotions: The available data does not mention active discount codes or seasonal promotions. Check FxPro's website or contact their sales team directly—they may offer account-opening bonuses or promotional spreads for new clients (these vary by region and time).

Conclusion: FxPro's zero-fee, multi-platform model offers genuine value for active forex and CFD traders, especially those using automation or seeking tight spreads. The real cost lies in spreads and commissions, not monthly fees. Spend time on the free demo, choose your account type based on trading frequency, and keep accounts active to avoid inactivity fees. For the right trader profile, FxPro competes favorably on price and features.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
